---
name: Render Auto-Deploy on Push

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      clear_cache:
        description: 'Clear build cache'
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean

jobs:
  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Render
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    steps:
      - name: Validate secrets
        id: secrets
        run: |
          MISSING=""
          if [ -z "${{ secrets.RENDER_API_KEY }}" ]; then
            echo "MISSING_SECRET: RENDER_API_KEY"
            MISSING="${MISSING} RENDER_API_KEY"
          fi
          if [ -z "${{ secrets.RENDER_SERVICE_ID }}" ]; then
            echo "MISSING_SECRET: RENDER_SERVICE_ID"
            MISSING="${MISSING} RENDER_SERVICE_ID"
          fi
          if [ -n "${MISSING}" ]; then
            echo "status=blocked" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "missing=${MISSING}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "::error::Missing secrets:${MISSING}"
          else
            echo "status=ready" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Trigger Render Deploy

- name: Trigger Render Deploy
        if: steps.secrets.outputs.status == 'ready'
        id: deploy
        run: |
          SERVICE_ID="${{ secrets.RENDER_SERVICE_ID }}"
          CLEAR_CACHE="${{ github.event.inputs.clear_cache == 'true' && 'clear' || 'do_not_clear' }}"

          echo "Triggering Render deploy for service: ${SERVICE_ID}"
          echo "GitHub SHA: ${{ github.sha }}"

          RESPONSE=$(curl -sS --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 30 \
            -X POST "https://api.render.com/v1/services/${SERVICE_ID}/deploys" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.RENDER_API_KEY }}" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -H "Accept: application/json" \
            -d "{\"clearCache\": \"${CLEAR_CACHE}\"}")

          echo "Render API response:"
          echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || echo "$RESPONSE"

          DEPLOY_ID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "
          import sys,json
          d = json.load(sys.stdin)
          deploy = d.get('deploy', d)
          print(deploy.get('id', ''))
          " 2>/dev/null || echo "")

          DEPLOY_STATUS=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "
          import sys,json
          d = json.load(sys.stdin)
          deploy = d.get('deploy', d)
          print(deploy.get('status', ''))
          " 2>/dev/null || echo "")

          echo "deploy_id=${DEPLOY_ID}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "deploy_status=${DEPLOY_STATUS}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

          if [ -n "${DEPLOY_ID}" ]; then
            echo "Deploy triggered: ${DEPLOY_ID} (${DEPLOY_STATUS})"
          else
            echo "::error::Could not parse deploy ID from Render response"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Wait for Deploy
        if: steps.deploy.outputs.deploy_id != ''
        id: wait
        run: |
          DEPLOY_ID="${{ steps.deploy.outputs.deploy_id }}"
          SERVICE_ID="${{ secrets.RENDER_SERVICE_ID }}"
          MAX_WAIT=600
          WAITED=0
          INTERVAL=10

          echo "Waiting for deploy ${DEPLOY_ID} to complete..."
          while [ $WAITED -lt $MAX_WAIT ]; do
            RESPONSE=$(curl -sS --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 15 \
              "https://api.render.com/v1/services/${SERVICE_ID}/deploys/${DEPLOY_ID}" \
              -H "Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.RENDER_API_KEY }}" \
              -H "Accept: application/json")

            STATUS=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "
            import sys,json
            d = json.load(sys.stdin)
            print(d.get('status', ''))
            " 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")

            COMMIT_SHA=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "
            import sys,json
            d = json.load(sys.stdin)
            commit = d.get('commit', {}) or {}
            sha = commit.get('id', '') if isinstance(commit, dict) else ''
            print(sha[:8] if sha else '?')
            " 2>/dev/null || echo "?")

            echo "[${WAITED}s] Status: ${STATUS} | Commit: ${COMMIT_SHA}"

            case "${STATUS}" in
              live)
                echo "Deploy is LIVE at commit ${COMMIT_SHA}!"
                echo "deploy_commit=${COMMIT_SHA}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

name: Render Deploy Trigger

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      clear_cache:
        description: 'Clear build cache'
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean

jobs:
  trigger-render-deploy:
    name: Trigger Render Deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    steps:
      - name: Check RENDER_API_KEY
        id: check-key
        run: |
          if [ -z "${{ secrets.RENDER_API_KEY }}" ]; then
            echo "RENDER_API_KEY secret is NOT configured in this GitHub repo."
            echo "status=missing_key" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "RENDER_API_KEY secret is configured."
            echo "status=key_found" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
          if [ -z "${{ secrets.RENDER_SERVICE_ID }}" ]; then
            echo "RENDER_SERVICE_ID secret is NOT configured."
            echo "service_id_missing=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "RENDER_SERVICE_ID secret is configured."
            echo "service_id_missing=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Trigger Render Deploy
        if: steps.check-key.outputs.status == 'key_found' && steps.check-key.outputs.service_id_missing == 'false'
        id: deploy
        run: |
          SERVICE_ID="${{ secrets.RENDER_SERVICE_ID }}"
          CLEAR_CACHE="${{ github.event.inputs.clear_cache == 'true' && 'clear' || 'do_not_clear' }}"
          
          echo "Triggering Render deploy for service: ${SERVICE_ID}"
          echo "Clear cache: ${CLEAR_CACHE}"
          
          RESPONSE=$(curl -sS --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 30 \
            -X POST "https://api.render.com/v1/services/${SERVICE_ID}/deploys" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.RENDER_API_KEY }}" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -H "Accept: application/json" \
            -d "{\"clearCache\": \"${CLEAR_CACHE}\"}")
          
          echo "Render API response:"
          echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || echo "$RESPONSE"

          DEPLOY_STATUS=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('deploy',{}).get('status','') or d.get('status',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
          
          echo "deploy_id=${DEPLOY_ID}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "deploy_status=${DEPLOY_STATUS}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

FROM oven/bun:1.2-alpine AS server-deps
WORKDIR /app

# NOTE: bun.lock is intentionally NOT required here. The GitHub sync pipeline
# (expo/sync-github.mjs) ignores bun.lock, so the lockfile in this repo can lag
# package.json (e.g. when a new dep like `unpdf` is added). `--frozen-lockfile`
# would then abort the build (the exact cause of prod being stuck on an old
# commit). Resolve fresh from package.json instead so new deps always install.
COPY package.json ./
RUN bun install --production

# Node 22 ships a native global WebSocket (Node 20 does not). @supabase/supabase-js
# initializes a Realtime WebSocket client in its constructor, so on Node 20 every
# createClient() call threw "Node.js 20 detected without native WebSocket support"
# before any owner-gated request could run. Node 22 (LTS) provides WebSocket natively.
FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app

# ffmpeg + ffprobe power the IVX video pipeline (HLS transcode ladder, thumbnails,
# posters) in backend/services/ivx-video-pipeline.ts and unlock the video worker
# (backend/services/ivx-video-worker.ts). Alpine's ffmpeg package ships both binaries.
#
# Chromium + nss/freetetype/harfbuzz/fontconfig power the IVX Browser Automation
# QA service (backend/services/ivx-browser-automation.ts). The service uses
# `playwright-core` (no bundled browser download) pointed at this system binary
# via PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_PATH, so the backend can produce real user-visible
# screenshots + DOM transcripts for owner-requested live QA. The --no-sandbox/
# --disable-setuid-sandbox flags are required because Render runs the container
# as non-root without seccomp privileges.
RUN apk add --no-cache ffmpeg chromium nss freetype harfbuzz ttf-freefont fontconfig noto-fonts-emoji
ENV PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_PATH=/usr/bin/chromium

ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOST=0.0.0.0

# NOTE: we deliberately do NOT copy the `bun` binary into this Node runner.
# A hard `COPY --from=server-deps /usr/local/bin/bun ...` is FATAL if the source
# path differs between bun image versions, which froze production on the last-good
# image (the landing-SEO auto-deployer never ran). The autonomous lifecycle's
# step 6 already degrades gracefully to the in-process tsx import-smoke check when
# bun is absent (ivx-runtime-resolver), so the Node-only runner is fully
# self-sufficient and the build can never fail on bun setup.
COPY --from=server-deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY package.json ./package.json
COPY server.ts ./server.ts
COPY tsconfig.json ./tsconfig.json
COPY backend ./backend
COPY expo/constants ./expo/constants
COPY expo/shared ./expo/shared

Found the root cause! The Dockerfile was changed between `f1eb2fb7` (working) and the current version. The key change: the Chromium install was made non-fatal (`|| echo "..."`) in the working version, but in the new version it's a hard `RUN apk add --no-cache ffmpeg chromium nss freetype harfbuzz ttf-freefont fontconfig noto-fonts-emoji` with no fallback.

If `ttf-freefont` or `noto-fonts-emoji` package names don't exist in the current Alpine version, the `apk add` fails and the entire Docker build fails. That's exactly what's happening — build fails in ~14-37 seconds during the `apk add` step.

Let me fix the Dockerfile to make the Chromium install non-fatal again, like the working version. This is the actual blocker preventing the signup form from going live.

The working version had:
```
RUN apk add --no-cache ffmpeg

RUN apk add --no-cache chromium nss freetype harfbuzz fontconfig font-noto-emoji || \
    echo "[IVX-QA] Optional Chromium install failed; QA service will run in status-only mode"
```

The broken version has:
```
RUN apk add --no-cache ffmpeg chromium nss freetype harfbuzz ttf-freefont fontconfig noto-fonts-emoji
```

The package name changes: `font-noto-emoji` → `noto-fonts-emoji`, and `ttf-freefont` was added. These may not exist in the current Alpine version, causing the build to fail.

# ffmpeg + ffprobe power the IVX video pipeline (HLS transcode ladder, thumbnails,

edits.newString:
# ffmpeg + ffprobe power the IVX video pipeline (HLS transcode ladder, thumbnails,
# posters) in backend/services/ivx-video-pipeline.ts and unlock the video worker
# (backend/services/ivx-video-worker.ts). Alpine's ffmpeg package ships both binaries.
RUN apk add --no-cache ffmpeg

# Chromium + deps power the IVX Browser Automation QA service. The install is
# intentionally non-fatal: if the Alpine package names/versions don't align with
# the current node:22-alpine base image, the container still builds successfully
# and the QA service degrades gracefully to status-only until the browser is
# available. The QA engine points to the system Chromium path and uses
# --no-sandbox / --disable-setuid-sandbox (required for the non-root Render runtime).
# NOTE: package names vary across Alpine versions (font-noto-emoji vs noto-fonts-emoji,
# ttf-freefont vs font-freefont). Try the known set, then a smaller fallback set.
RUN apk add --no-cache chromium nss freetype harfbuzz fontconfig font-noto-emoji font-freefont ttf-freefont noto-fonts-emoji 2>/dev/null \
    || apk add --no-cache chromium nss freetype harfbuzz fontconfig font-noto-emoji 2>/dev/null \
    || apk add --no-cache chromium nss freetype harfbuzz fontconfig noto-fonts-emoji 2>/dev/null \
    || apk add --no-cache chromium nss freetype harfbuzz fontconfig 2>/dev/null \
    || echo "[IVX-QA] Optional Chromium install failed; QA service will run in status-only mode"
ENV PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_PATH=/usr/bin/chromium

-- =====================================================================
-- IVX Member Signup — picture_url column
-- =====================================================================
-- Adds an optional profile picture URL to every member record.
--
-- HOW TO APPLY (one time, ~10 seconds, NO redeploy needed)
--   1. Open Supabase dashboard -> your project -> SQL Editor -> New query.
--   2. Paste this entire file and click Run.
--
-- This script is idempotent (safe to run more than once). The backend
-- reaches these tables via the service-role key, which bypasses RLS.
-- =====================================================================

-- 1. profiles.picture_url  (Supabase Auth user profile row) -------------
alter table public.profiles
  add column if not exists picture_url text default '';

comment on column public.profiles.picture_url is
  'Optional URL to the member profile picture in the member-pictures storage bucket.';

-- 2. members.picture_url  (canonical members registry) ------------------
alter table public.members
  add column if not exists picture_url text default '';

comment on column public.members.picture_url is
  'Optional URL to the member profile picture in the member-pictures storage bucket.';

-- 3. member-pictures storage bucket -------------------------------------
-- Public read so signup pictures are visible in the app + admin module.
insert into storage.buckets (id, name, public)
  values ('member-pictures', 'member-pictures', true)
  on conflict (id) do update set public = true;

-- 4. Storage policies (allow authenticated uploads, public reads) -------
drop policy if exists "member-pictures public read" on storage.objects;
create policy "member-pictures public read"
  on storage.objects for select
  using (bucket_id = 'member-pictures');

drop policy if exists "member-pictures authenticated upload" on storage.objects;
create policy "member-pictures authenticated upload"
  on storage.objects for insert
  to authenticated
  with check (bucket_id = 'member-pictures');

drop policy if exists "member-pictures anon upload" on storage.objects;
create policy "member-pictures anon upload"
  on storage.objects for insert
  to anon
  with check (bucket_id = 'member-pictures');
